data.name: CF-Gateway-DNSTunnel — DNS Tunneling / C2 Beaconing (Gateway DNS)
data.description: Behavioural detection of DNS tunneling, DGA/C2 beaconing, and data-in-DNS exfiltration on Cloudflare Gateway DNS logs (class_uid 4003). Groups queries by source host and registered-zone proxy (rightmost 3 labels of unmapped.QueryName) and fires on: (a) >=15 unique subdomains under one zone (DGA/beaconing), (b) >=5 queries with a >=40-char leftmost label AND >=5 unique labels (dnscat2/iodine long-label tunneling), (c) >=3 TXT queries whose leftmost label is >=20 chars (base32 data-in-DNS exfil), or (d) >=10 high-entropy (digit-rich) labels AND >=5 unique labels. Every branch requires clustering, not a single long label — a lone long label maxes at the 63-char DNS spec limit and cannot be distinguished from legit SaaS/CDN hosts (the standalone max_label_len clause was removed after a validated false positive on saas.atlassian.com). Does not hard-code any domain. MITRE: T1071.004 (Application Layer Protocol: DNS), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), related T1568.002 (DGA). Covers OneFlare scenario 05 (attack-scripts/scenarios/05_dns_tunnel.py). Cloudflare source: run the asset-enrichment solution so device.uid binds the Target Asset.

scheduledParams.query: class_uid=4003 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='Gateway DNS' unmapped.QueryName=* | let fqdn = lower(unmapped.QueryName) | let label = replace(fqdn, '\\..*', '') | let zone = replace(fqdn, '^.*\\.([^.]+\\.[^.]+\\.[^.]+)$', '$1') | let label_len = len(label) | let digit_cnt = len(replace(label, '[^0-9]', '')) | let digit_ratio = label_len > 0 ? digit_cnt / label_len : 0 | let is_txt = query.type in:anycase ('txt') | let src_ip = src_endpoint.ip | let host = device.name | group total_queries = count(), uniq_labels = estimate_distinct(label), max_label_len = max(label_len), long_labels = count(label_len >= 40), txt_long = count(is_txt AND label_len >= 20), hi_entropy = count(label_len >= 12 AND digit_ratio >= 0.15), device_uid = any(device.uid), evidence = max_by(unmapped.QueryName, label_len), first_seen = oldest(timestamp), last_seen = newest(timestamp) by src_ip, host, zone | filter uniq_labels >= 15 OR (long_labels >= 5 AND uniq_labels >= 5) OR txt_long >= 3 OR (hi_entropy >= 10 AND uniq_labels >= 5) | let reason = uniq_labels >= 15 ? 'DGA / high unique-subdomain volume' : (txt_long >= 3 ? 'Data-in-DNS exfil (TXT long labels)' : (long_labels >= 5 ? 'Long-label DNS tunneling' : 'High-entropy label cluster')) | sort -uniq_labels | limit 100
